criteria.label: Distinguish line (%) from cell (%%) magics and use common ones.
criteria.example: `%timeit sum(range(1000))` (line magic — applies to one expression) vs `%%timeit\n[x*x for x in range(1000)]` (cell magic — applies to the whole cell). Pattern: `%` for one line, `%%` for the whole cell.

criteria.label: Use %pip for installs and %env for session config correctly.
criteria.example:
```
%pip install langchain          # installs into THIS kernel
%env OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...      # sets env var for this session
```
Never use `!pip install` — it may target a different Python.

criteria.label: Benchmark with %timeit/%time and inspect state with %whos.
criteria.example: `%timeit chunk_text(doc)` runs it many times, reports a stable mean ± stdev. `%time chunk_text(doc)` runs once (good for slow ops). `%whos` lists every variable currently in the kernel — handy for spotting leaks between experiments.

criteria.label: Navigate edit/command modes with keyboard shortcuts and use autoreload.
criteria.example: Command mode (Esc): `A`/`B` insert cell above/below, `DD` delete, `M` to markdown, `Y` to code. Edit mode (Enter): type normally. For editing imported modules without restarting: `%load_ext autoreload; %autoreload 2`.
